title: Module 6.3: Outdoor Hazard Calculations (SAE/ANSI)
description: Comprehensive guide to calculating NOHD, SZED, CZED, and LFED using SAE/ANSI methodologies with interactive calculation tools and real-world examples.

learning_objectives.objective: Calculate NOHD using Equation 70-1.1 and 70-1.2 for CW and pulsed lasers
Apply Visual Correction Factors for multi-wavelength calculations
Calculate SZED, CZED, and LFED using the fixed multipliers
Convert slant range to horizontal and vertical distances using trigonometry
Apply MPE correction factors (CA, CC) for near-infrared wavelengths

sections.title: MPE Determination
sections.content_summary: Using Tables 1 and 2 from FAA AC 70-1B to determine Maximum Permissible Exposure values for various wavelengths and exposure durations.

sections.title: Safety Distance Calculator
sections.content_summary: Comprehensive calculation tool for NOHD, SZED, CZED, and LFED with step-by-step formula display and validation.

sections.title: Visual Correction Factors
sections.content_summary: Calculating Pre-corrected Power (PCP), Visual Correction Factor (VCF), and Visually Corrected Power for visible lasers.

sections.title: Multi-Wavelength Calculations
sections.content_summary: Calculating combined hazards for RGB and multi-wavelength lasers using Equation 70-1.14.

sections.title: Real-World Examples
sections.content_summary: Worked examples from FAA AC 70-1B Appendix B and C.

questions.question: What is the NOHD slant range for a 40W CW visible laser with 1.5 mrad divergence?
What is the fixed multiplier to calculate CZED from SZED?
questions.explanation: The CZED slant range = SZED_SR × 4.47 (from Equation 70-1.4)

questions.question: What is the Visual Correction Factor (VCF) for 555 nm wavelength?
questions.explanation: 555 nm is at the peak of human photopic vision, so VCF = 1.0
